name: HOPOPT
description: Used to carry optional information that must be processed by every node along the path of an IPv6 packet.

name: ICMP
description: Used for error reporting and network diagnostics. Carries messages such as echo request/reply (ping), destination unreachable, and time exceeded.

name: IGMP
description: Used by IP hosts and adjacent routers to establish and manage multicast group memberships on a local network.

name: IPv4
description: IP-in-IP tunneling; encapsulates an IPv4 packet within another IPv4 packet for tunneling or VPN purposes.

name: TCP
description: Provides reliable, ordered, and error-checked delivery of a stream of bytes between applications. Used by HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, SSH, and most connection-oriented protocols.

name: EGP
description: An obsolete inter-AS routing protocol used to exchange reachability information between autonomous systems. Superseded by BGP.

name: IGP
description: A reserved protocol number for any interior gateway routing protocol used within an autonomous system.

name: UDP
description: A simple, connectionless protocol that sends datagrams without establishing a connection or guaranteeing delivery. Used by DNS, DHCP, VoIP, video streaming, and gaming.

name: RDP
description: Provides reliable delivery of datagrams with sequencing and acknowledgement. Not to be confused with Microsoft Remote Desktop Protocol (which uses TCP/UDP port 3389).

name: IPv6
description: Carries IPv6 packets inside IPv4 packets for tunneling across IPv4-only infrastructure during IPv6 transition.

name: IPv6-Route
description: An IPv6 extension header that allows an IPv6 source to list intermediate nodes to be visited on the way to the destination.

name: IPv6-Frag
description: An IPv6 extension header used by the source node to send packets larger than the path MTU by fragmenting them.

name: RSVP
description: A signaling protocol used to reserve network resources along a path for Quality of Service (QoS) guarantees.

name: GRE
description: Encapsulates a wide variety of network layer protocols inside IP tunnels. Widely used for VPNs, PPTP, and overlay networks.

name: ESP
description: Provides confidentiality, data origin authentication, integrity, anti-replay protection, and limited traffic flow confidentiality as part of the IPsec suite.

name: AH
description: Provides connectionless data integrity, data origin authentication, and optional anti-replay protection for IP packets as part of the IPsec suite.

name: ICMPv6
description: The IPv6 equivalent of ICMP. Used for error reporting, diagnostics, and critical IPv6 functions including Neighbor Discovery and Router Solicitation.

name: IPv6-NoNxt
description: Indicates that there is no next header following the current IPv6 extension header. Used when an IPv6 packet carries no payload.

name: IPv6-Opts
description: An IPv6 extension header carrying optional information to be processed only by the packet's destination node.

name: OSPF
description: A link-state interior gateway routing protocol that uses Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest paths. Widely used in enterprise and ISP core networks.

name: PIM
description: A family of multicast routing protocols that build multicast distribution trees independent of any particular unicast routing protocol.

name: IPComp
description: Reduces the size of IP datagrams by compressing the payload. Often used in conjunction with IPsec to improve throughput.

name: VRRP
description: Provides automatic assignment of available routers to participating hosts, increasing the availability and reliability of default gateways.

name: L2TP
description: A tunneling protocol used to support VPNs and deliver ISP services. Does not provide encryption on its own; usually paired with IPsec.

name: SMP
description: A lightweight message-passing protocol assigned to IP protocol number 121, with limited documented deployment.

name: SCTP
description: A reliable, message-oriented transport protocol supporting multi-streaming and multi-homing. Used in telephony signalling (SS7 over IP) and WebRTC data channels.

name: UDPLite
description: A variant of UDP providing optional partial checksum coverage, useful for multimedia applications where some data corruption is preferable to packet loss.

name: MPLS-in-IP
description: Encapsulates MPLS label stacks in IP packets for transport over IP-only networks without requiring MPLS-capable infrastructure.

name: Ethernet
description: Encapsulates Ethernet frames within IP packets, enabling Ethernet-over-IP tunneling for LAN extension across IP networks.

name: Experimentation-253
description: Reserved for use in experimentation and testing. Must not appear in production traffic and should never be assigned to any protocol specification.

name: Experimentation-254
description: Reserved for use in experimentation and testing. Must not appear in production traffic and should never be assigned to any protocol specification.
